title: Lemon Herb Quinoa
description: Light, bright, and protein-forward — works as a side or base. Scales easily for batch cooking.

instructions.text: Combine quinoa and water or broth in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a low simmer, cover, and cook until liquid is absorbed, about 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and let sit covered for 5 minutes — this finishes the steam and keeps it fluffy, not mushy.
While quinoa rests, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice and zest, and minced garlic.
Fluff quinoa with a fork, pour dressing over, and fold in parsley and mint or basil. Season with salt and pepper. Serve warm or at room temperature.

notes:
Use broth instead of water for more depth. Mint gives it a fresher feel; basil is earthier — either works.

Keeps well refrigerated for 3–4 days, making it a good Sunday batch-cook candidate. Add a handful of arugula or spinach folded in at the end if you want more greens in the meal.
